segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What is the writer's carpet made of?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者的地毯是由什么做成的？

segments.text: We have just moved into a new house, and I have been working hard all morning.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们刚搬进一所新房子，我整个上午一直在辛苦干活。

segments.text: I have been trying to get my new room in order.

translations.zh-Hans: 我一直在设法把新房间整理好。

segments.text: This has not been easy because I own over a thousand books.

translations.zh-Hans: 这并不容易，因为我有一千多本书。

segments.text: To make matters worse, the room is rather small, so I have temporarily put my books on the floor.

translations.zh-Hans: 更糟的是，房间相当小，所以我暂时把书放在了地板上。

segments.text: At the moment, they cover every inch of floor space, and I actually have to walk on them to get in or out of the room.

translations.zh-Hans: 眼下，书铺满了地面的每一寸空间，我进出房间实际上都得踩着它们走。

segments.text: A short while ago, my sister helped me to carry one of my old bookcases up the stairs.

translations.zh-Hans: 不久前，姐姐帮我把一个旧书架搬上了楼。

segments.text: She went into my room and got a big surprise when she saw all those books on the floor.

translations.zh-Hans: 她走进我的房间，看到地上那些书时大吃一惊。

segments.text: This is the prettiest carpet I have ever seen, she said.

translations.zh-Hans: “这是我见过的最漂亮的地毯，”她说。

segments.text: She gazed at it for some time, then added, You don't need bookcases at all.

translations.zh-Hans: 她凝视了一会儿，然后又说：“你根本不需要书架。

segments.text: You can sit here in your spare time and read the carpet.

translations.zh-Hans: 闲暇时你可以坐在这里读这张地毯。”
